  • Prior leasing, sales, or customer service experience
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Basic accounting knowledge and comfort handling financial transactions
  • Familiarity with Fair Housing Laws
  • Weekend availability
  • Entrata Core experience is a plus

Cottonwood offers a quarterly bonus structure on top of base pay, which means your performance has a direct line to your compensation. Benefits kick in within the first 30 days, and the PTO package in year one (120 hours plus 10.5 paid holidays) is competitive for the industry. The 401k and HSA company match add up over time and are worth factoring into the total picture.

Fort Worth's multifamily market has stayed active, and Cottonwood's portfolio gives you a stable platform to build real operational skills. If community manager is the next step you're working toward, this role gives you the exposure to get there.

job.title: Maintenance Technician
job.description:
Maintenance technicians at apartment communities carry more responsibility than most job postings let on. You're not just fixing things. You're the person residents see when something has gone wrong in their home, which means your attitude and follow-through shape how they feel about living there. At Richmond Apartments in Millcreek, that dynamic is central to the role.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Cottonwood Residential is bringing on an experienced Maintenance Technician to support day-to-day operations at their Richmond Apartments community in Salt Lake City. The work spans the full range of residential maintenance: plumbing repairs, electrical troubleshooting, HVAC service, and general carpentry. You'll move through units handling make-readies, responding to resident service requests, and keeping the property grounds in good shape.

HVAC experience matters here. Cottonwood specifically calls out considerable troubleshooting experience, and EPA Type II or Universal certification is required. A CPO certification is a plus if you have it. You'll also need a valid driver's license and the physical stamina the job demands: lifting up to 50 lbs, working on ladders and scaffolding, and spending most of your shift on your feet.

On-call availability is part of the deal. After-hours emergencies happen, and you'll be expected to respond with the support of the Service Manager. If you've done this before, you know that "on-call" weeks are manageable when the team is organized and communication is clear. That's worth asking about in your interview.

What Separates Good Candidates Here

Technical skills get you the interview. What actually makes someone successful in this kind of role is the combination of solid troubleshooting instincts and the ability to communicate clearly with residents who are frustrated or anxious about a repair. Being able to explain what you did, why it took the time it did, and what to expect next goes a long way toward resident retention. That's not soft skills fluff. It directly affects how communities perform.

  • EPA Type II or Universal certification (required)
  • Considerable HVAC troubleshooting experience
  • CPO certification (preferred)
  • Valid driver's license
  • Availability for on-call and after-hours emergency response
  • Ability to read and interpret safety and operating manuals in English

The Company and What's Offered

Cottonwood Residential has a real footprint in the Utah market and has built a reputation for operational innovation, including being among the first apartment managers to roll out self-guided tours at scale. The benefits package is competitive: full health, vision, dental, life, and disability coverage available within the first 30 days, a 401k and HSA with company match, 10.5 paid holidays, and three weeks of PTO in your first year. That last point is worth noting. Three weeks of paid time off in year one is genuinely above average for a maintenance role in this industry.

If you're a technician who wants to grow, this type of role builds the cross-trade experience that leads to Service Manager positions over time. Strong technicians who also develop vendor coordination skills and learn to manage a punch list efficiently tend to move up faster than those who stay narrowly focused on one trade.

job.title: Community Manager
job.description:
Running a multifamily community at the Class A level is a different job than people expect. Yes, there's leasing and maintenance coordination, but the real work is holding together a team, a budget, and a resident experience simultaneously, often on the same Tuesday morning. Waypoint Management Services is hiring a Community Manager in Jacksonville to do exactly that.

What This Role Actually Covers

You'll own the full operational picture. That means daily property operations, resident lifecycle management from move-in through renewal, vendor relationships, and making sure the team is hitting their marks on occupancy and collections. Delinquency doesn't manage itself, and rent growth doesn't happen without someone watching the market closely and adjusting strategy accordingly.

On the financial side, you'll handle budget preparation, capex oversight, and performance reporting. Waypoint expects you to read the numbers and act on them, not just forward a report. You'll also conduct regular market surveys and competitive shops to make sure pricing and concessions stay sharp relative to what's happening across Jacksonville's submarket.

Team leadership is a core part of the job. You'll recruit, coach, and hold accountable a team of leasing and maintenance professionals. Building a culture where people actually want to perform, and want to stay, takes real effort. This role expects that effort.

What Waypoint Is Looking For

  • At least 3 years of experience specifically in a property manager seat (not just assistant-level)
  • 5 or more years total in multifamily, with Class A community experience
  • Proven ability to lead and develop teams, not just manage tasks
  • Strong financial background including budgeting, accounts receivable and payable, and capital project management
  • Solid knowledge of Fair Housing regulations, leasing procedures, and maintenance operations
  • Proficiency in Yardi and CRM platforms
  • A genuine feel for marketing strategy and closing techniques, not just posting to ILS platforms and hoping

A bachelor's degree is preferred but not a strict requirement if your experience is strong.

What Separates Strong Candidates Here

Jacksonville's multifamily market has seen meaningful supply growth over the past few years, with new Class A product continuing to deliver across the Southside, St. Johns County corridor, and parts of the urban core. That means residents have options, and retention strategy matters as much as traffic generation. The managers who do well in this environment know how to read a T-12, identify where NOI is leaking, and also walk a prospect through a model in a way that actually converts. Both skills matter. If you've only lived on one side of that equation, this role will push you.

Compensation includes a quarterly manager bonus, monthly leasing and renewal bonuses, a competitive base wage, and an employee housing discount. Benefits including medical, dental, and vision coverage start on day one. PTO starts at 120 hours in year one and grows with tenure. There's also a 401k with company match, commuter benefits, and an employee referral program.

job.title: Concierge (M-F, 4pm-12am) - Lease-up
job.description:
It's 6:30pm on a Tuesday and three things are happening at once: a prospect is walking through the front door for a self-guided tour, a resident needs the loading dock reserved for a move-in tomorrow morning, and the amenity room calendar has a conflict nobody caught. That's a normal evening in this role.

Comstock is hiring a Concierge for the evening shift (Monday through Friday, 4pm to midnight) at a lease-up community in Reston, VA. This is a Class A environment, and the expectation matches that. During lease-up, foot traffic is higher, resident expectations are being set for the first time, and the front desk carries more weight than it will once the community stabilizes. The impression you make in these early months shapes how residents talk about the building for years.

What the Role Actually Covers

Your core job is keeping the front-of-house running cleanly during the busiest hours of the day. That means greeting residents, guests, and prospects as they come through, managing access so only authorized individuals enter, and keeping the reception area sharp and organized. You'll field incoming calls and route them correctly, coordinate amenity space reservations and loading dock scheduling, and do daily building walks to catch maintenance issues, reset furniture, and clean up anything that slipped through earlier in the day.

Because this is a lease-up, you're also working alongside leasing staff during a period when occupancy is actively climbing. You won't be pulling rent rolls or working delinquency, but you will be a visible, daily touchpoint that either reinforces or undermines what the leasing team is selling. Strong concierges at lease-up properties understand that distinction.

What Comstock Is Looking For

  • High school diploma or GED required
  • At least one year of concierge or high-end customer service experience preferred
  • Comfortable with Microsoft Office, especially Outlook, Word, and Excel
  • Able to work weekends and holidays as needed, with flexibility to pick up additional shifts
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • High integrity, accountability, and the kind of professionalism that doesn't need supervision to show up
  • Genuine ability to connect with a diverse group of people without making it feel like a performance

The candidates who stand out in this type of role aren't just friendly. They're organized under pressure, they notice things before being told, and they treat every resident interaction like it's the first one of the day, even at 11:45pm. That consistency is hard to train.

Comstock offers a competitive salary with bonus potential, medical, dental, vision, 401(k) match, life insurance, a housing discount, commuter benefits, free parking with EV charging, parental leave, and annual professional development funds. The company has an expanding portfolio, which means this role has a realistic path upward for someone who performs well and wants to grow in property management.

job.title: Property Manager
job.description:
This role runs on a specific combination of skills: leasing instincts, financial awareness, vendor coordination, and the kind of interpersonal steadiness that keeps residents from escalating small issues into big ones. If those four things describe how you work, this position is worth a close look.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Property Management Associates is hiring a full-time, live-on-site Property Manager for a portfolio of 6 residential properties totaling 58 units in Beverly Hills. All properties sit within a mile of each other, which means your commute between sites is a short drive or walk. That geographic tightness is genuinely useful. You're not burning half your day in traffic between disconnected properties.

The job touches everything. You're walking properties daily, working through preventive maintenance checklists, conducting move-in and move-out inspections, and keeping punch lists from growing stale. On the financial side, you're monitoring the monthly budget and overseeing accounts payable and receivable. Leasing is a significant part of this role. You'll be expected to actively market vacancies, develop strategies to drive traffic, and close leases. Sitting on vacancies isn't an option here.

You'll also supervise maintenance staff and manage outside vendors, which means your scheduling and communication skills get used constantly. Daily contact with senior management and property owners is part of the structure, so written and verbal clarity matters. Yardi Voyager experience is required. MS Office proficiency is expected.

The Honest Part

Living on-site has real advantages: a free one-bedroom apartment is included, along with a $300 monthly auto allowance. The business office operates out of your apartment. At $23 to $25 per hour for 40 hours per week, the compensation picture is reasonable when you factor in the housing benefit.

That said, living where you work is a genuine lifestyle consideration. Residents know where you live. You'll work one weekend day per week. After-hours situations come with the territory on any residential portfolio, and a small portfolio doesn't eliminate that reality. A valid driver's license, auto insurance, and the ability to pass a background check, DMV check, and pre-employment physical are all required before starting.

What Strong Candidates Bring

  • 2 to 4 years of hands-on property management experience, ideally in residential
  • Proven leasing ability, not just familiarity with the process
  • Working knowledge of Yardi Voyager
  • Experience scheduling and supervising maintenance staff and vendors
  • Comfort with basic financial oversight at the property level
  • Strong written communication for owner and resident correspondence

What separates a good candidate from an average one here is probably leasing confidence combined with operational follow-through. A lot of property managers are stronger on one side than the other. This role genuinely needs both. PMA has been in operation for over 30 years and describes itself as a company that values an entrepreneurial mindset and invests in its staff. For a self-directed manager who wants real ownership of a small, concentrated portfolio in one of LA's most desirable neighborhoods, that combination has genuine appeal.

job.title: Maintenance Technician
job.description:
Maintenance Technician roles at owner-operated multifamily communities carry a different weight than third-party managed properties. When the same company owns and manages the asset, there's genuine accountability to the physical condition of the building, and the maintenance team sits at the center of that. At Berkshire Residential's 20 Midtown community, that's exactly the setup you're walking into.

What This Role Actually Involves

You're responsible for the day-to-day physical health of the property. That means responding to service requests across the usual range: plumbing, electrical, appliances, carpentry, and general building systems. But it also means being an active set of eyes on the community. Curb appeal isn't just a leasing concern. It affects resident satisfaction, retention, and the overall condition of the asset over time.

Beyond the work orders, you'll support inventory control, equipment maintenance, and key security in the shop. You'll help enforce safety standards consistently and back up management in maintaining community policies. The role requires you to communicate clearly with residents and teammates alike, so strong written and verbal English is a practical requirement here, not a formality.

  • At least one year of hands-on maintenance experience in a residential community setting
  • Working knowledge of plumbing, electrical, appliances, carpentry, and general building maintenance
  • Comfort with computers and basic digital tools for logging work orders or communication
  • Ability to work independently while staying aligned with team and management priorities

What This Role Builds

If you're earlier in your maintenance career, working at an owner-operator like Berkshire is a genuine accelerator. Because the company manages its own assets, you're exposed to how physical condition decisions connect to NOI and long-term asset value. That's context a lot of maintenance technicians don't get until they're much further along. Technicians who develop strong diagnostic instincts here, meaning the ability to spot a problem before it becomes a work order, tend to move into lead tech or maintenance supervisor roles faster than peers who only react to requests.

Berkshire describes its culture as people-first, with an emphasis on individual development. The company offers personal development plans alongside their benefits package, which suggests there's actual structure around career growth rather than just a talking point in a job posting. For a maintenance professional who wants to grow beyond the wrench, that structure matters.

A Note on Fit

The strongest candidates for this kind of role aren't just technically capable. They're consistent. A great maintenance technician at a community like this is the person residents recognize and trust, someone whose work closes tickets completely the first time and who treats a resident's service request with the same care on a Friday afternoon as on a Monday morning. That reliability is harder to train than any technical skill, and it's what Berkshire is really looking for when they talk about going the extra mile.

job.title: Sales & Resident Experience Consultant
job.description:
What You'll Actually Do

At Tinsley on the Park, the leasing consultant role sits at the intersection of sales performance and resident experience. Your mornings might start with walking tour routes and model units, checking that everything looks sharp before traffic picks up. From there, it's a mix of inbound inquiries, scheduled tours, and follow-up calls with prospects who are still weighing their options. Berkshire holds consultants to at least a 20% closing ratio, so this isn't a role where showing apartments is enough. You need to understand what's driving someone's decision and help them get there.

Market awareness matters here too. Knowing what competing communities are offering, what concessions are in play, and where Tinsley on the Park stands relative to them gives you real credibility when a prospect pushes back. You'll also contribute ideas to your property manager on marketing and resident satisfaction, which means your observations from the leasing floor actually feed into broader strategy.

Day-to-day admin is part of the job. Resident communications, written follow-ups, and keeping the leasing office running with a professional atmosphere all require genuine attention to detail. The "people-first" side is real, but so is the paperwork.

What You Bring

  • At least two years of customer-facing experience, whether that's hospitality, retail, or prior leasing work
  • A genuine comfort with the sales process, including handling objections and closing
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Adaptability with technology, since leasing platforms and CRM tools change often
  • High energy and consistency across busy and slow traffic days alike

The Craft You'll Build Here

Leasing consultants who develop real sales discipline, not just friendliness, tend to move into assistant manager roles faster than their peers. The closing ratio expectation at Berkshire is a training ground for that. You'll sharpen your ability to read a prospect's timeline, tailor a pitch to their actual priorities, and track your own performance in a measurable way. Those are skills that transfer directly into assistant management, where you're overseeing delinquency, renewals, and occupancy goals rather than just traffic conversion.

Berkshire owns and manages its own properties, which means you're working for an integrated team rather than a third-party management shop. That structure tends to mean more consistency in standards, more investment in people, and more room to grow within the same organization if you perform. Benefits include three weeks of vacation, a 25% rent discount, personal development plans, and health insurance.

job.title: Assistant General Manager
job.description:
Property management companies at the mid-to-upper tier of multifamily operations run on the strength of their second-in-command. The General Manager sets the vision, but the Assistant General Manager is the one who actually keeps the day-to-day from unraveling. Bozzuto is hiring for that role in Fort Lauderdale.

This is a leasing-forward, operations-grounded position at what Bozzuto operates as a premium residential community. You're not just covering the front desk when the GM is out. You're driving occupancy through personalized tours, managing delinquency, posting receipts, and coaching your team through the small moments that add up to resident retention. Fort Lauderdale's rental market is competitive, with strong demand from young professionals and a steady churn of residents who have real options. That means your leasing instincts matter. A tour that feels scripted won't close.

What the Role Actually Looks Like

On a typical day, you might start by reviewing delinquency and following up on outstanding balances, then shift to walking the property to make sure the curb appeal and common areas meet Bozzuto's standard before afternoon traffic picks up. You'll take tours yourself, not just delegate them. When the GM is off-site, the team looks to you. That means vendor conversations, resident escalations, and coaching a leasing consultant through a difficult interaction all fall in your lap, sometimes in the same hour.

Weekend availability is part of the role. One to two weekends per month, with corresponding weekdays off. That's the honest reality of leasing-driven property management, and it's worth knowing upfront.

What Bozzuto Is Looking For

  • A four-year degree or equivalent hands-on experience in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • At least two years working in one of those industries, with real exposure to rent collection, accounts receivable, and basic budget awareness
  • Comfort with Yardi or comparable property management software
  • Genuine ability to connect with people in conversation, not just on paper
  • Active presence on social media platforms, with the instinct to create content that actually represents a community well
  • A current real estate license, or the willingness to obtain one within 90 days of hire where required
  • A polished, professional appearance that reflects the brand

The salary range sits between $60,000 and $66,000, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401(k) with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

Strong candidates for this role typically have one quality that separates them from the rest: they're equally comfortable closing a lease and having a hard conversation with a resident about a late payment. Both require trust-building, and both happen on the same shift. If you lean heavily toward one and avoid the other, this role will feel unbalanced quickly.

For someone who performs well here, the natural next step is a General Manager or Property Manager role, either within Bozzuto's portfolio or across the broader multifamily industry. The AGM seat is genuinely one of the best training grounds in the business.

job.title: Assistant Maintenance Manager
job.description:
It's 7:15 on a Saturday morning and a resident has no heat. Your phone buzzes, you run through the likely culprits in your head before you've even had coffee, and by 9 a.m. the issue is resolved and documented. That's the job, and if that scenario sounds more energizing than exhausting, keep reading.

What This Role Actually Involves

Bozzuto is looking for an Assistant Maintenance Manager at one of their Alexandria, VA communities. The title says "assistant," but the scope is real. You're co-running the maintenance operation: turns, preventative maintenance, emergency response, and the day-to-day service ticket queue. You'll work hands-on with HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair while also keeping an eye on the broader team's performance and professionalism.

The make-ready process is a significant part of this. Turns need to come back clean, complete, and on schedule so leasing can do its job. A slow or sloppy punch list shows up in occupancy numbers fast. You'll own that cycle alongside the maintenance manager and be expected to hold the team to a consistent standard without the leasing office having to chase you for updates.

Preventative maintenance isn't optional here. Bozzuto operates well-maintained, higher-end communities, and asset preservation is taken seriously. If you're comfortable with complex mechanical systems like central plant, boilers, or chillers, that background will be useful. It's not required, but it separates candidates who can grow into a senior role from those who top out early.

Weekend availability and participation in the on-call rotation are real expectations. Worth saying plainly: this role does not have clean 9-to-5 edges.

What You Need to Bring

  • At least 5 years in the trades, facilities, or residential building maintenance
  • HVAC, EPA, and/or NAPE certification
  • Working knowledge of plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Basic carpentry, drywall, and paint skills for make-ready work
  • Familiarity with OSHA standards, risk management, and MSDS protocols
  • Some supervisory experience or a demonstrated ability to lead a small crew
  • Comfort with technology platforms and basic Microsoft Office proficiency
  • Physical ability to handle the demands: lifting, ladders, stairs, and outdoor work in all weather

The salary range sits between $66,000 and $69,000, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

One thing worth noting about Bozzuto specifically: they promote from within more consistently than most operators we've seen at their scale. An Assistant Maintenance Manager who performs well here has a credible path to Maintenance Manager and eventually to a Regional Facilities role. The company tracks certifications and development, so if you're willing to keep building your credentials, the trajectory is there.

job.title: Assistant Maintenance Manager
job.description:
Strong technical skills drive this role. So does the ability to lead a team, manage a punch list, and keep a property running without cutting corners. The Assistant Maintenance Manager position at Bozzuto in Brandywine, MD puts both to work every day.

The Skills This Role Runs On

HVAC is the backbone. You need real hands-on experience with split systems, boilers, chillers, and central plant equipment, not just a passing familiarity. EPA certification matters here. So does working knowledge of plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair. Bozzuto is looking for someone who can diagnose a problem, fix it correctly, and close the ticket without a second trip.

Beyond the technical side, this is a supervisory role. You're not just doing the work. You're modeling how the work gets done. That means coaching your team on safety standards, OSHA compliance, and MSDS handling while keeping the operation accident-free. A safety mindset isn't optional here. It's the baseline.

The make-ready process is another core skill area. Managing turns efficiently, keeping units on schedule, and getting apartments rent-ready without sacrificing quality directly affects the property's occupancy. Delays in turns mean delayed move-ins. That pressure is real, and you'll own it.

What You'll Actually Be Doing

Day to day, you're splitting time between resident service tickets and broader asset preservation. A resident's AC goes down, you handle it. The preventative maintenance schedule needs to run on time, you make sure it does. Snow removal, grounds upkeep, and general cleanliness fall under your watch too. None of it is glamorous, but all of it matters for the property's condition and resident satisfaction.

You'll also be in the on-call rotation and available for weekend work. That's a real commitment. If you've worked in residential property maintenance before, this won't surprise you. But it's worth stating plainly: this role doesn't clock out at 5.

  • 5 or more years in the trades, facilities, or residential building maintenance
  • HVAC, EPA, and/or NAPE certification required or strongly preferred
  • Supervisory or lead experience is a plus
  • Comfort with Microsoft Office and property maintenance software platforms
  • Ability to lift 25 to 50 pounds frequently, climb ladders, and work in varying weather
  • Basic carpentry, drywall, and painting skills

What Sets Strong Candidates Apart

The technicians who stand out in this kind of role aren't just skilled with tools. They think about the building the way an owner would. Bozzuto uses the phrase "know it like you built it," and that's the right mental frame. The best candidates have an instinct for catching problems before they become work orders, and they communicate clearly enough that residents trust them and team members follow their lead.

Bozzuto is a well-regarded operator with a reputation for high standards across its portfolio. Coming in as an Assistant Maintenance Manager here gives you exposure to professional systems and processes that translate directly if you move toward a Maintenance Manager or Chief Engineer role down the road. The salary range for this position is $61,000 to $65,000, with bonus eligibility, benefits including medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Assistant Property Manager
job.description:
Bozzuto runs some of the better-managed communities in the country, and their Assistant Property Manager role reflects that. This isn't a leasing-only position with a fancy title. You're genuinely second in command, expected to own the financial side, support the team, and step into the Property Manager seat when needed.

What This Role Actually Covers

The financial responsibilities here are real. You'll handle rent collection, manage delinquency, and maintain awareness of the community's P&L. If you've only ever done leasing and toured prospects around, this role will stretch you. That's not a bad thing, but go in with clear eyes.

On the leasing side, you're conducting tours and converting traffic into signed leases. Bozzuto expects those tours to feel personalized, not scripted. You're also responsible for maintaining the physical presentation of the community, inside and out, and making sure the team projects the same level of professionalism. Social media content creation is part of the job too, specifically Instagram and Facebook, so comfort with that is genuinely expected, not just a checkbox.

Weekend availability is part of the deal. One to two weekends per month, with compensating days off during the week. That's standard for most communities doing any real leasing volume, and Kirkland is an active submarket.

What You'll Need

  • A four-year degree or equivalent experience in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • At least two years in a relevant customer-facing or operations role
  • Some exposure to budgets, revenue tracking, or P&L management
  • Proficiency with Yardi or similar property management software
  • Solid written and verbal communication skills
  • Real sales ability, not just friendliness

Worth Knowing Before You Apply

The APM role is one of the cleaner paths to a Property Manager title in this industry. You're building the exact skill set required: financial oversight, team leadership, leasing performance, and operations. At a company like Bozzuto, that progression is more structured than you'd find at a smaller owner-operator. The salary range sits between $54,080 and $58,240, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of PTO plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

If you've been doing leasing for a couple of years and you're ready to stop handing off the hard conversations to someone else, this is the step up that actually teaches you the business.

job.title: Assistant Property Manager
job.description:
What You'll Actually Do

This role sits at the intersection of leasing, operations, and resident relations. As an Assistant Property Manager at Bozzuto's West Harrison community, you're the person keeping the wheels turning when the Property Manager is out, and a key contributor when they're in. Your days will shift between reviewing delinquency reports, following up on outstanding balances, and walking a prospective resident through a personalized tour that actually converts. Those two things require completely different headspace, and you'll switch between them constantly.

The financial side is real here. You're expected to understand occupancy, rent collection, and how delinquency affects the property's bottom line. You're not just handing off problems to someone else. You own them. On the leasing side, your tours need to be tailored, not scripted. Residents in the West Harrison area are often commuters with high expectations, and generic pitches don't close leases in this market.

You'll also coach the team around you. That means setting the tone on how residents are greeted, how maintenance requests get communicated, and how the community presents itself physically. Curb appeal and team appearance aren't afterthoughts at Bozzuto. They're part of the job.

Weekend availability is part of this position. One to two weekends per month, with corresponding weekdays off. That's when leasing traffic peaks, and Bozzuto wants leadership on-site for it.

What You Bring

  • A four-year degree or equivalent experience in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • At least two years working in one of those industries, with real exposure to budgets and P&L
  • Comfort with Yardi or similar property management software
  • Strong written and verbal communication, because you're corresponding with residents, vendors, and ownership-level contacts
  • Active social media skills, specifically the ability to create content on platforms like Instagram and Facebook that reflects the community's brand
  • A real estate license, or the willingness to obtain one within 90 days of hire where required
  • Genuine sales instincts. Not pressure tactics. The ability to listen, match a prospect to the right home, and close.

The Bigger Picture

Strong APMs at companies like Bozzuto tend to move into Property Manager roles within two to three years. The skills you build here, particularly financial oversight, team leadership, and resident retention, transfer directly into that next step. This isn't a leasing consultant role with a new title. It carries actual responsibility for community performance.

The honest reality: this role requires you to stay calm when a resident is upset about a maintenance delay, firm when someone is 45 days past due on rent, and warm when a prospect walks in on a busy Saturday. Those three things can happen in the same hour. If you've worked in hospitality or retail management before, that rhythm will feel familiar. If you haven't, expect a real adjustment period.

Bozzuto offers a salary range of $75,000 to $80,000 for this position, plus bonus eligibility. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Assistant Property Manager
job.description:
It's a Tuesday afternoon and the property manager is out. A prospective resident just walked in without an appointment, two maintenance work orders are sitting unresolved, and rent collection for the month is three days from closing. That's a normal Tuesday as an Assistant Property Manager at Bozzuto's Miami community.

What This Role Actually Involves

The APM position at Bozzuto sits at the intersection of sales, operations, and resident relations. You're not just a leasing agent with a fancier title. You own the delinquency process, meaning you track who hasn't paid, follow up consistently, and help protect the community's NOI. You conduct tours, and Bozzuto expects those tours to be personalized and informed, not scripted and robotic. You coach and mentor the team around you, which requires patience and genuine follow-through, not just enthusiasm.

When the Property Manager is out, you lead. Full stop. That responsibility is baked into the role, and it's worth taking seriously before you apply.

Weekend availability is required, one to two weekends per month, with compensating weekdays off. That trade-off works well for some people and poorly for others. Worth being honest with yourself about it upfront.

Bozzuto uses Yardi, so comfort with that platform matters. Social media content creation is also part of the job, specifically Facebook and Instagram, so if you've never thought about how a property presents itself online, you'll want to start.

What You Bring

  • A four-year degree or equivalent experience in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • At least two years working in one of those industries, with real exposure to budgets and P&L
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Comfort with industry software, Yardi preferred
  • A Florida real estate license, or a willingness to obtain one within 90 days of hire
  • A genuine aptitude for sales, not just a tolerance for it

The salary range for this role runs from $55,000 to $59,000, with additional bonus opportunities. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

One thing worth noting about the APM role specifically: it's one of the cleaner stepping stones in residential property management. APMs who develop strong financial fluency alongside their people skills tend to move into Property Manager roles faster than those who stay purely on the leasing side. The delinquency and budget exposure you get here translates directly to what's expected at the next level.

What separates strong candidates from average ones in this role isn't charm or sales energy. It's the ability to stay organized under pressure while managing multiple priorities that all feel urgent at the same time. Miami is a high-traffic rental market with competitive lease-up conditions, which means the pace here is real and the expectations for resident experience are high.

job.title: Evening Concierge
job.description:
What You'll Actually Do

This role runs Monday through Friday, 11am to 8pm, which means you're the face of the building during the hours residents actually use it. You're not managing a back-office inbox. You're greeting people coming home from work, handling the wave of packages that stacks up every afternoon, and solving whatever comes up before the evening shift ends. Small stuff and not-so-small stuff, back to back.

Bozzuto operates a portfolio of high-quality residential communities, and at this San Francisco property the concierge desk functions as the heartbeat of the resident experience. You'll plan and execute community events that actually get people to show up, not just calendar invites that get ignored. You'll manage front desk logs, coordinate club room and freight elevator reservations, and keep the package room from descending into chaos. You'll also help build the community's online reputation by encouraging satisfied residents to share their experiences on review platforms.

The social media piece is real work here. Bozzuto communities use social channels to generate leasing interest and keep current residents engaged, and you'll contribute to that effort with content that reflects the energy of the property.

What You Bring

  • At least two years working in hospitality, hotel, retail, or travel, where reading people quickly and responding gracefully was part of the job
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, including comfort with digital tools, apps, and property software
  • A professional presence and genuine attention to detail
  • The ability to anticipate what someone needs before they ask for it
  • Physical ability to lift up to 25 pounds (package volume gets real during the holidays)

Context Worth Knowing

San Francisco's luxury residential market is competitive, and residents in Class A buildings have high expectations baked in from day one. The concierge role at this level isn't just about being friendly. It's about consistency. Residents notice when the experience slips, and they notice when it's excellent. Your ability to remember names, preferences, and even pets by name is the kind of detail that drives renewal decisions and five-star reviews more than any amenity upgrade can.

The 11am to 8pm window is worth thinking about honestly. You'll catch the tail end of move-ins, the full afternoon delivery surge, and the early evening resident return. It's a high-touch shift. People are tired after work and want things handled quickly and warmly. If you've thrived in a hotel front desk or concierge role where the afternoon-to-evening transition is the busiest stretch, that experience maps directly here.

The salary range for this position is $55,000 to $58,000, with bonus eligibility, benefits including medical, dental and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Evening Concierge (Tuesday-Saturday, 3-11pm)
job.description:
It's 6pm on a Thursday. A resident walks through the lobby after a long commute, carrying groceries and looking frazzled. You greet them by name, hand them the package that arrived that afternoon, and mention that the rooftop event this Saturday starts at 7. They leave the lobby smiling. That's the job, distilled.

What This Role Actually Involves

Bozzuto is hiring an Evening Concierge for a residential community in Hackensack, NJ. The shift runs Tuesday through Saturday, 3pm to 11pm, which means you own the building's most active hours: commuter arrivals, package rushes, evening amenity use, and weekend energy. You're the face residents see most often.

The responsibilities span a wider range than most people expect from a concierge title. Yes, there's front desk work: managing logs, coordinating freight elevator reservations, keeping the package room organized (especially during the holidays, when volume spikes hard). But there's also genuine community-building work. You'll plan and execute resident events, manage social media presence to build visibility, and actively encourage satisfied residents to share their experiences online. The role blends hospitality, light marketing, and operational detail into a single position.

Bozzuto describes its culture around four values: concern, creativity, passion, and the pursuit of perfection. Those aren't just wall art. A company that builds, manages, and maintains its own communities tends to have more internal consistency than a third-party operator juggling dozens of unrelated clients. That matters for a concierge role because brand standards stay stable.

What You Bring

  • At least two years working in hospitality, retail, hotel, or travel environments
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Comfort with software, apps, and property-related computer systems
  • A detail-oriented mindset and a habit of anticipating needs before they're voiced
  • Professional appearance and a genuine interest in people
  • Ability to lift up to 25 pounds for package handling

The salary range is $48,000 to $50,000 annually, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

One thing worth knowing: concierge roles at well-run residential communities are legitimate entry points into property management careers. The skills you build here, reading residents, managing competing priorities under time pressure, executing events, maintaining a brand standard consistently, translate directly into leasing consultant and assistant manager roles. If you're thinking about where this leads, the path is real and well-worn at companies like Bozzuto that promote from within.

job.title: Executive Concierge
job.description:
Luxury residential communities live and die by the quality of their front-of-house experience. When a prospect walks through the lobby at a Bozzuto property in Cambridge, the Executive Concierge is often the first human impression they get. That moment either converts or it doesn't. This role exists to make sure it converts, and then keeps residents happy enough to renew.

Bozzuto is hiring an Executive Concierge at their Cambridge, MA community. The base salary runs $55,000 to $58,000, with additional bonus opportunities on top of that. This is not a pure front desk job. You're part operator, part hospitality lead, and part team coach. When the property manager or assistant PM isn't on site, you step into that leadership gap without being asked.

What the Day Actually Looks Like

Your shift might open with a package room that needs organizing before the morning rush, a follow-up call to a resident whose maintenance request went sideways, and a quick check on the club room reservation log before a weekend event. By mid-morning you're greeting prospects by name if you've met them before, and working to learn new names fast if you haven't. Resident retention is a real KPI here. The relationship you build with a resident in month two is what influences whether they renew in month eleven.

Bozzuto takes resident events seriously as a retention tool, and you'll plan and execute them. You'll also manage the property's social media presence, including encouraging happy residents to post reviews publicly. Those reviews feed traffic. Traffic feeds occupancy. It all connects.

Weekends and evenings are part of the rotation, depending on your assigned shift. That's worth knowing upfront.

What Bozzuto Needs From You

  • At least 2 years in hospitality, retail, hotel, or a similarly service-intensive environment
  • Genuine comfort with social media content creation on platforms like Instagram and Facebook
  • Strong written and verbal communication, and a professional appearance that matches a luxury building
  • Comfort with software, apps, and property management tools
  • Ability to lift up to 25 pounds (package volume gets real during the holidays)
  • An instinct for anticipating what someone needs before they ask

What separates a strong candidate from an average one here isn't just friendliness. It's the ability to hold relationship detail in your head. Knowing a resident's dog's name, remembering that another one travels every other week and needs packages held, noticing when a prospect seems hesitant and knowing how to reopen that conversation naturally. That kind of attentiveness doesn't come from a checklist. It has to be genuine.

Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. Bozzuto also offers employee resource groups and invests meaningfully in internal career development, which matters if you're thinking about where this role leads. Executive Concierge experience at a well-run Bozzuto community is a real stepping stone toward leasing consultant or assistant property manager positions for people who want to grow in residential management.

job.title: Floating Maintenance Technician
job.description:
The Role in Context

Floating maintenance roles are operationally demanding in a specific way. Instead of owning one community's punch list, you're moving across multiple properties, picking up wherever the gap is. That means your diagnostic speed matters. You don't have weeks to learn a building's quirks. You need to walk in, assess, and execute.

Bozzuto operates across a portfolio of residential communities, and this technician position supports that network from a Chapel Hill base. The work spans the full maintenance spectrum: service ticket resolution, make-ready turns, preventative maintenance, and after-hours emergency response when something breaks at 11pm and a resident is waiting.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Day to day, you're resolving maintenance issues across HVAC, electrical, plumbing, and appliance repair. Beginner to intermediate proficiency covers the baseline. You're also contributing to turns, which means prepping vacant units for move-in through repairs and improvements that directly affect lease-up readiness and resident satisfaction scores. Grounds upkeep, snow removal, and general building cleanliness round out the scope.

  • HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair at a beginner to intermediate level
  • Turn and make-ready work on vacant units
  • Preventative maintenance and general upkeep
  • On-call rotation for after-hours emergencies
  • Weekend and evening availability as needed
  • Physical capability to frequently move 25 to 50 pounds
  • Personal, reliable transportation (required for on-call response)
  • Comfort with software and mobile apps used for work orders

Strong candidates here aren't necessarily the most experienced technicians in the room. What separates them is response reliability and communication. A resident-facing maintenance role lives or dies on follow-through. If you close a ticket cleanly, communicate clearly, and show up on call without drama, you'll stand out fast.

Growth and Compensation

The base salary is $48,000, with additional bonus opportunities on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. One specific perk worth noting: after your 12-month anniversary, Bozzuto covers your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification in full. For anyone looking to sharpen their HVAC credentials without paying out of pocket, that's a real tangible benefit.

From a career trajectory standpoint, floating technician roles build something most single-property positions don't: cross-portfolio exposure. You see how different communities are operated, where processes break down, and how maintenance coordinators and property managers interact with maintenance under pressure. That breadth is often what accelerates a move into a lead technician or maintenance supervisor role down the line.

job.title: Leasing Consultant
job.description:
Leasing is a relationship business. Prospects aren't just shopping for square footage; they're deciding where they want to live their daily life, and the person who earns their trust during a tour often determines whether they sign. That's the work at the center of this role.

Bozzuto is hiring a Leasing Consultant at their Bayonne, NJ community. Bozzuto manages properties with a distinct emphasis on resident experience, and that standard runs all the way through the leasing process. You're the first real impression of the community for most prospects, and the expectation is that every interaction reflects that.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Your days will move between follow-up outreach, community tours, and social media content. Converting leads into tours means staying on top of your pipeline, responding promptly, and knowing how to share information about both the apartment and the surrounding Bayonne neighborhood in a way that feels helpful rather than scripted. Tours need to be personalized. Generic walkthroughs don't close leases; conversations about how a specific floor plan fits a prospect's actual life do.

The social media piece is real work here, not an afterthought. You'll be expected to create and post content on platforms like Instagram and Facebook that builds genuine interest in the community. If you've only managed a personal account, that's worth thinking through. This role calls for a consistent, on-brand presence.

The schedule includes three weekends per month, with two weekdays off in exchange. That's the standard trade-off in leasing, and it's worth understanding upfront. Weekends are when traffic peaks, so that's when you need to be there.

What Sets Strong Candidates Apart

  • Customer-focused communication skills, both written and verbal, with genuine follow-through on prospect inquiries
  • Comfort with leasing software, CRM tools, and basic computer programs
  • A professional appearance and the self-awareness to read a prospect's priorities quickly during a tour
  • Real experience creating social media content, not just consuming it
  • Sales instincts that don't feel pushy. The best leasing consultants guide decisions rather than pressure them

Candidates who stand out tend to have a natural curiosity about people. They ask good questions. They remember details from earlier in a conversation and bring them back at the right moment. That's what turns a tour into a lease and a lease into a long-term resident relationship.

Compensation is set at $51,500 with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. For someone early in a property management career, the leasing consultant path at a company like Bozzuto can move toward assistant manager and property manager roles with real operational depth behind it.

job.title: Leasing Consultant
job.description:
Bozzuto has built a real reputation in the mid-Atlantic and Southeast for operating communities where the resident experience actually matches the marketing. That standard doesn't maintain itself. It depends on people at the leasing desk who treat every walk-in, every phone call, and every follow-up email as something worth doing well.

This Leasing Consultant role at their Chapel Hill community is the front line of that experience. You're the first real conversation most prospective residents have with the property. Your job is to convert genuine curiosity into signed leases, not through pressure, but through knowing the community well enough to help someone picture their life there. That means following up with leads consistently, conducting tours that feel personal rather than scripted, and understanding that the difference between a good tour and a forgettable one is usually just how much you listened.

The social media piece is real work here, not an afterthought. Bozzuto expects you to actively build awareness and interest through platforms like Instagram and Facebook. If you've got a knack for creating content that actually gets engagement rather than just filling a feed, that skill matters in this role.

One honest note about the schedule: this position requires working three weekends per month, with two weekdays off in exchange. That's standard for leasing roles because weekends are when traffic peaks and serious prospects show up. If you've worked retail, hospitality, or any customer-facing role with weekend hours, you already know the rhythm. If you haven't, it's worth thinking through before you apply.

What separates strong leasing consultants from average ones isn't really sales technique. It's follow-through. The lead who toured on Saturday and hasn't responded to your Tuesday email is still a potential resident. Knowing when to follow up, how often, and what to say each time is a skill that takes time to develop, and it's what drives occupancy numbers more than any single great tour.

A real estate license is either required or needs to be obtained within 90 days of hire where applicable. Bozzuto offers competitive hourly pay in the $21 to $24 range plus bonus opportunities, along with medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of PTO plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

What you'll bring

  • Strong written and verbal communication, the kind that holds up in email, in person, and on the phone
  • Comfort with social media content creation, specifically Instagram and Facebook
  • Sales instincts grounded in listening rather than pitching
  • Competency with software, leasing platforms, and general tech tools
  • A professional, put-together presence that reflects the community's brand
  • Real estate license or willingness to obtain one within 90 days

Chapel Hill is a competitive rental market with steady demand driven by UNC and a growing professional population. Prospects here tend to ask good questions and compare options carefully. Knowing the neighborhood well enough to speak to it honestly is part of doing this job right.

job.title: Leasing Consultant
job.description:
Bozzuto operates across the full property management spectrum, from development through day-to-day operations, which means a leasing role here isn't isolated work. You're a visible part of a larger machine, and your ability to convert traffic into signed leases has a direct, measurable effect on occupancy and NOI at the community level.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Your core job is turning interest into leases. That means following up with prospects, answering questions about the community and the surrounding Kent area, and conducting tours that feel personal rather than scripted. You're not just showing floor plans. You're helping someone picture their life in a specific place, and that requires you to listen before you pitch.

Social media is part of the role too. You'll create content for platforms like Instagram and Facebook to build awareness and keep the community visible to people who are just starting their apartment search. This isn't a side task. It's a real part of how modern lease-ups and stabilized properties generate traffic without depending entirely on ILS spending.

The schedule runs three weekends per month, with two weekdays off in exchange. That's worth knowing upfront. Weekends are when most prospective residents are actively looking, so your availability during that window is directly tied to your ability to close.

What Bozzuto Expects You to Bring

  • Strong written and verbal communication, including comfort with digital correspondence
  • A customer-first approach and genuine interest in helping people find the right home
  • Comfort with social media platforms and a willingness to create original content
  • Sales instincts, not pressure tactics, but the ability to move a conversation toward a decision
  • Proficiency with software, apps, and property management tools
  • A polished, professional presence

Where This Role Can Take You

Leasing consultant is the foundational role in residential property management, and the skills you build here compound quickly. The best consultants develop an instinct for reading prospects, handling objections, and closing under pressure. Those same skills translate directly into assistant manager and property manager roles, where you're also managing delinquency, renewals, and concession strategy. Bozzuto specifically invests in development through tuition reimbursement and career programs, which makes this a more viable long-term move than a typical leasing job at a smaller operator.

What separates strong candidates from average ones in this role isn't raw sales ability. It's follow-through. Prospects who don't hear back quickly rarely convert. If you're the type who closes the loop without being reminded, you'll stand out fast.

job.title: Leasing Consultant
job.description:
Bozzuto operates at the higher end of the multifamily spectrum, and their leasing roles reflect that. This isn't a position where you hand someone a floor plan and hope for the best. It's a consultative sales role where relationship-building and storytelling drive results.

What This Role Actually Involves

As a Leasing Consultant (titled internally as Sales and Marketing Associate), you'll own the full top-of-funnel experience for prospective residents. That means converting inbound leads into scheduled tours, following up consistently with prospects, and conducting personalized tours that speak to the specific lifestyle a community offers. Generic pitches don't close here. Understanding the neighborhood, the floor plan differences, and what actually makes a resident feel at home is what separates a strong month from a slow one.

Beyond the tour itself, you'll contribute to the community's social media presence, creating content on platforms like Instagram and Facebook that builds awareness and keeps the brand visible between prospect visits. Schedule-wise, expect to work three weekends per month, with two weekdays off in exchange. That rhythm is standard in leasing because that's when traffic peaks. If you've done this before, you already know it.

  • Follow up with all leads and provide compelling, accurate information about the community and surrounding area
  • Conduct tours tailored to each prospect's priorities, with the goal of converting visits into signed leases
  • Support resident retention by maintaining a consistently positive experience
  • Create and post social media content that reflects the community's identity
  • Work comfortably across leasing software, apps, and digital communication tools

What You Bring

Strong written and verbal communication is non-negotiable. You're comfortable on camera or in a caption, and you present yourself professionally in person. Sales aptitude matters here, but the best leasing consultants at companies like Bozzuto tend to be genuinely curious about people, not just closing-focused. Residents can tell the difference, and so can renewal rates.

The role pays between $47,000 and $50,000 base, with additional bonus opportunities on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

Where This Role Can Take You

Leasing Consultant is one of the most transferable starting points in property management. The skills you build here, qualifying prospects, handling objections, learning how to read a delinquency report and connect it to occupancy trends, directly prepare you for an Assistant Manager role. From there, the path to Property Manager is a matter of adding financial fluency and team oversight. Bozzuto's size and portfolio mean internal mobility is a real option, not just something mentioned in an interview. If you're thinking about where this leads, that's the right question to be asking.

job.title: Leasing Consultant
job.description:
The Skills That Drive This Role

Leasing is a conversation-first job. Before contracts, before applications, before keys, there's a person standing in front of you trying to picture their life in a new home. The skill that matters most here isn't sales technique. It's the ability to read what someone actually needs and respond to it honestly. Bozzuto's leasing approach leans into that, and this position in Osprey reflects it.

You'll spend a meaningful portion of your week on follow-up. Converting initial leads into scheduled tours requires persistence without being pushy, and that's a real skill. Strong written communication matters just as much as verbal here because a lot of those touchpoints happen over email, text, or social media before a prospect ever walks through the door.

Social media fluency is genuinely part of the job description, not a soft add-on. If you're comfortable creating content on Instagram or Facebook that reflects a community's personality rather than just broadcasting availability, that transfers directly to what Bozzuto is asking for. It's a small but meaningful part of building traffic organically.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Most of your week will rotate between three things: nurturing leads, conducting tours, and supporting the overall resident experience. Tours here are expected to be personalized, which means you'll need to know the community and the surrounding Osprey area well enough to speak to both. Residents and prospects often ask about schools, commute routes, nearby dining, and lifestyle fit. Generic answers lose leases.

Weekend availability is real here. Three weekends per month is the expectation, with two weekdays off in exchange. That's a standard trade-off in leasing, but it's worth knowing going in. Prospects actively search for apartments on Saturdays and Sundays, so that's when your presence matters most.

  • Converting leads into scheduled and completed community tours
  • Conducting personalized tours that reflect the resident's specific lifestyle needs
  • Building and maintaining positive relationships that support lease renewal and brand loyalty
  • Creating and posting social media content to generate interest in the community
  • Maintaining professional communication across all written and verbal interactions
  • Working comfortably across multiple software platforms and leasing tools

What Makes a Strong Candidate Here

The difference between an average leasing consultant and a strong one usually comes down to follow-through and adaptability. Anyone can give a good tour on a good day. The people who build real leasing track records are consistent: they follow up when it's inconvenient, they stay personable when traffic is slow, and they don't let a lost lease affect the next conversation.

Bozzuto puts genuine weight on culture and resident experience across its portfolio. That means they're looking for someone who takes pride in the details, not someone who treats leasing as a purely transactional role. If you've worked in hospitality, retail, or customer-facing service and found yourself genuinely enjoying the human side of it, that background translates well here.

The salary range for this position is $47,000 to $50,000 annually, with bonus opportunities on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. Career-wise, strong leasing consultants at companies like Bozzuto often move into assistant manager or leasing manager roles, where the same communication and relationship skills apply at a broader operational level.

job.title: Maintenance Manager
job.description:
The Skills This Role Lives On

Maintenance management at Bozzuto is fundamentally a leadership role that happens to require deep technical fluency. You're not just fixing things. You're teaching others how to fix things, building the systems that prevent things from breaking, and making judgment calls that protect both the resident experience and the asset value of the property.

The technical foundation matters a great deal here. Advanced HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair aren't nice-to-haves; they're the credibility that earns a team's respect and allows you to diagnose problems quickly, assign work intelligently, and know when a vendor is padding scope. Exposure to complex mechanical systems like boilers, chillers, and central plant equipment is a genuine differentiator for this position. If you've worked in garden-style communities your whole career, the systems complexity here may be a stretch worth acknowledging honestly.

Equally important is the supervisory craft. With 3+ years of team leadership expected, Bozzuto is looking for someone who has already made the mental shift from individual technician to multiplier. That means you've hired people you're proud of, coached techs through difficult repairs rather than just handling them yourself, and built a team culture where accountability is normal rather than punitive.

What You'll Be Building and Protecting

Day to day, this role operates across several distinct functions. The make-ready and turnover process demands attention to both speed and quality. Every day a unit sits vacant is lost revenue, so your ability to manage a punch list efficiently and keep turns on schedule directly affects NOI. Preventative maintenance programs protect the long-term value of the asset. Vendor management keeps capital improvement projects on budget. And emergency on-call coverage means the role doesn't fully clock out at 5pm, which is an honest reality worth understanding upfront.

  • 7+ years of facilities or residential building maintenance experience
  • 3+ years leading maintenance teams, including hiring and development
  • HVAC, EPA, and/or NAPE certification
  • Working knowledge of OSHA, risk management, and MSDS standards
  • Comfort with Microsoft Office and property operations technology platforms
  • Physical ability to lift 25 to 50 pounds frequently and work in varying weather conditions
  • Availability for emergency response and on-call rotation

What Strong Candidates Bring That Average Candidates Don't

The people who thrive in this role tend to think like owners. They track recurring work orders and ask why a repair keeps coming back. They build vendor relationships before they need them urgently. They treat a resident's service ticket as a promise, not a task in a queue. Bozzuto uses the phrase "know it like you built it," and that mentality is genuinely useful here because the best maintenance managers develop an almost institutional knowledge of their property's quirks, histories, and vulnerabilities.

This role also builds skills that transfer well across the industry. Managing capital projects sharpens budgeting instincts. Leading a team through a high-volume turn season develops operational planning skills. Both are directly relevant if your long-term interest is facilities director or regional maintenance oversight. Bozzuto's investment in tuition reimbursement and career development reflects a company that takes that trajectory seriously.

The compensation for this position is $72,800, paired with bonus eligibility, health, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, and a 401k with company match.

job.title: Maintenance Technician
job.description:
Skills This Role Runs On

This Maintenance Technician position at Bozzuto's Sunnyvale, CA community puts four skill sets to work every single day: HVAC troubleshooting, plumbing, electrical repair, and appliance work. You don't need to be a master of all four on day one. Beginner to intermediate proficiency gets you in the door. What matters is that you're comfortable diagnosing problems, completing repairs correctly, and moving on to the next ticket without a lot of hand-holding.

The schedule runs Sunday through Thursday, so Friday and Saturday are your weekend. That's worth knowing upfront because it shapes your life outside work as much as it shapes your shifts.

What You're Actually Doing Day to Day

Service ticket resolution is the core of the job. Residents submit requests, you resolve them. HVAC calls, leaky faucets, tripped breakers, broken appliances. You work through the queue, document your work, and keep residents informed. That last part matters more than people expect. A repair done right but communicated poorly still creates a bad experience.

Beyond tickets, you're turning vacant units for move-in. Make-ready work: punch list repairs, touch-ups, getting the apartment into showing condition. It's detail-oriented work that directly affects how quickly a unit can be leased.

You're also part of an on-call rotation for after-hours emergencies. That's a real commitment. Reliable personal transportation isn't just a preference here, it's a hard requirement so you can respond when something breaks at 11pm.

Grounds upkeep and general building cleanliness round out the role. The physical demands are real too. Expect to lift and carry between 25 and 50 pounds regularly throughout a shift.

What Separates Strong Candidates Here

Bozzuto operates Class A communities and holds service quality to a high standard. The technicians who do well here aren't just mechanically competent. They communicate clearly with residents, show up looking professional, and treat every interaction as part of the resident experience, not a distraction from the repair work.

One practical upside worth knowing: after your 12-month anniversary, Bozzuto covers the cost of your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification. For someone early in their HVAC career, that's a meaningful credential paid for by the company rather than out of your own pocket. It also signals something about how this employer approaches technician development.

  • Experience in building maintenance or a trades education background
  • Beginner to intermediate HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair skills
  • Availability for on-call emergency response, including weekends and evenings
  • Reliable personal vehicle
  • Strong written and verbal communication
  • Basic computer skills for work order software and apps
  • Ability to lift 25 to 50 pounds frequently

Compensation for this role sits between $54,110 and $58,000 annually, with bonus eligibility on top of base pay. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Maintenance Technician
job.description:
The Skills That Drive This Role

Maintenance tech work at a Bozzuto community in Lake Worth comes down to a specific combination of technical range and people skills. You're not a specialist here. You're handling HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair across a single property, which means your value is in breadth. Beginner-to-intermediate proficiency across those trades is the baseline. What separates a good tech from a great one is the ability to triage quickly, communicate clearly with residents, and keep the punch list moving without constant supervision.

Turns matter here too. Preparing vacant units for move-in is a real part of the job, and how efficiently you move through make-ready work affects the property's ability to lease. A tech who treats turns as an afterthought tends to create bottlenecks at exactly the wrong time.

What the Day Actually Looks Like

You'll work through a service ticket queue, handle preventative maintenance tasks, support grounds upkeep, and jump into turns when units come available. Bozzuto properties are known for a higher standard of presentation, so general cleanliness and building aesthetics are part of your responsibility, not just an occasional ask. This is South Florida, so HVAC calls aren't seasonal. They're year-round, and resident tolerance for a down A/C unit is low. You should expect that reality.

On-call rotation is part of the deal. After-hours emergencies happen, and you'll need reliable personal transportation to respond. That's not unique to Bozzuto, but it's worth saying plainly: this isn't a strictly 9-to-5 position.

What Bozzuto Brings to the Table

One thing worth noting for techs who are still building their credentials: Bozzuto will cover the cost of your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification after your first year. That's a meaningful credential in this market, and having it paid for by your employer is a genuine benefit, not a throwaway line. Salary for this role falls between $48,000 and $50,000, with bonus eligibility on top of that.

Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of PTO plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. The culture at Bozzuto gets mentioned a lot in our industry, and it's not just marketing. They've built a reputation for investing in their maintenance staff in ways that a lot of fee-management shops simply don't.

  • Experience in building maintenance or a trades education
  • Beginner to intermediate HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair skills
  • Availability for on-call rotation and emergency response
  • Reliable personal transportation
  • Strong written and verbal communication
  • Comfort with basic computer use and property software
  • Ability to frequently lift and move 25 to 50 pounds

If you're a tech who takes pride in the quality of your work and wants to build credentials while doing it, this is a solid place to land.

job.title: Maintenance Technician
job.description:
Bozzuto has a reputation in this industry for running tight communities and investing in the people who keep them running. This Maintenance Technician opening in Fort Lauderdale is a ground-level role in the truest sense: you're the person residents call when something goes wrong, and you're the reason a turned unit looks ready for a new resident to sign a lease and move in with confidence.

The work is what you'd expect from a solid mid-size property management company. Service ticket resolution, HVAC, plumbing, electrical, appliance repair. Turns and make-readies. Grounds upkeep. You'll also carry your share of the on-call rotation, which means the occasional evening or weekend call-out is part of the deal. That's not a small thing. If after-hours availability is a dealbreaker for you, this role isn't the right fit. If you're already used to it, you know it's just part of working in residential maintenance.

One thing worth noting: Bozzuto will pay for your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification after your first year. For a technician who doesn't yet have it, that's real money and a real credential. It opens doors to more complex HVAC work and, frankly, to higher-paying roles down the road. Companies that pay for certifications are showing you something about how they treat their maintenance staff.

What they're looking for

  • Some background in building maintenance or a trades education, beginner to intermediate skill level across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Ability to handle on-call emergencies and participate in the rotation
  • Physical capability to lift and exert force regularly throughout a shift (25 to 50 lbs frequently)
  • Reliable personal transportation for getting to the property and responding after hours
  • Comfort with basic software and mobile apps used for work orders and maintenance tracking
  • Clear written and verbal communication, professional presentation

The salary range sits between $51,000 and $53,000, plus bonus eligibility. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

What separates a strong candidate here isn't just technical skill. It's the combination of solid hands-on ability and genuine customer service instincts. In residential property management, a technician who fixes things correctly and leaves the resident feeling heard is worth considerably more to an operation than one who only does half of that. Bozzuto properties tend to skew toward quality communities where resident experience is taken seriously. That culture starts with who they put in front of residents every day.

job.title: Maintenance Technician
job.description:
It's 7 a.m. on a Monday. A resident submitted a ticket over the weekend: no hot water. You're the first person they see, and how you handle the next 30 minutes sets the tone for their entire week.

Bozzuto is hiring a Maintenance Technician for their community in Burke, VA. This is a hands-on, full-cycle maintenance role covering everything from daily service requests to unit turns and grounds upkeep.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Most of your day runs on service tickets. HVAC, plumbing, electrical, appliance repair. You diagnose the problem, fix it correctly, and close it out. Residents notice when you do it fast and when you do it right. Both matter here.

Unit turns are a regular part of the job. When a resident moves out, you go in, assess the punch list, and get the apartment ready for the next move-in. You're not just patching walls. You're resetting the unit to a standard that justifies the rent.
